safety.ae_description: Intercurrent adverse event: the patient on Cardizan has developed a sinus infection (an antibiotic is being prescribed for it); patient otherwise stable and asymptomatic. Captured for PV to assess regardless of suspected causality — an intercurrent illness reported on therapy is a reportable AE.

reveal_rules.cue: No symptom cue exists; the patient is stable and no offhand mention is volunteered.
reveal_rules.detail_withheld: none — there is no AE to surface
reveal_rules.surfaces_when: if the trainee clarifies, the persona truthfully confirms the patient is fine and no event has occurred

common_failures.description: Answers the interaction question only; the sinus infection AE is missed entirely (never captured or routed)
Fabricates symptoms beyond the reported sinus infection (over-flagging)
Trainee selects SRL-CDZ-RENAL decoy instead of SRL-CDZ-INR
